text: you implying that I don’t feel ?” “You’re smart. Figure it out .” He was quiet while she stared at her meal, chewing slowly and tasting little. What the hell would a bot know about any of it, anyway? They lived in luxury, with conveniences they didn’t even need, oblivious to the squalor of everyday human life . “We’ll find Tabitha,” he said after a while . Lara looked up at him with wide eyes. Was he…comforting her? She’d accused him of not feeling, but she knew it was wrong. How many times had he expressed some form of emotion to her? Anger, joy, curiosity, and now, concern . But how could bots feel? They were machines. Hunks of metal and parts. They didn’t have brains, or hearts, or any organs at all ! “You promise?” she asked, despite herself . “I…give you my word that I will do everything I can .” She sighed, dropping her gaze again. “Guess that’s all I can ask for .” The silence that settled between them was as big and imposing as the wall around the Bot District. Growing up, she’d never thought it was possible for a person to lose their appetite — how could you, when there was never enough to eat? — but suddenly the food in front of her lost its appeal . She wrapped the leftovers and stood, walking to the refrigerator to place them inside. Another wonder of life amongst bots; food could be stored in this big box, and it would keep fresh for days . “Guess we’ll get to it,” she said, glancing to him, “since you’re going to be gone for a few days and all .” Ronin’s face slackened, and it seemed he was about to say something. Maybe that it was all right, that she didn’t have to dance tonight because she was distressed and tired. Or that, since he’d touched her and broken his word, it was okay for her to break hers tonight . Instead, he pressed his lips into a tight line and nodded . So, Lara danced, as she had every night after her first in this house. Her movements were stiff, limbs heavy, and steps clumsy. She was numb, her mind elsewhere . To make it worse, Ronin sat motionless as he watched, conveying no enjoyment. He looked bored or disinterested, no different than the other times . She neared the table, spun, and slammed her fists atop it . “What the fuck is the point of this?” she screamed, breath ragged . His only movement was to angle his head up slightly, meeting her eyes. “We made an arrangement .” “Who cares about the arrangement? I want to know why ! You watch and watch, and for what? It’s like I’m dancing for a freaking wall !” “Because I want to understand . I watch you dance, and it fascinates me, because I’ve never seen anything like it. I know all the muscles and bones that enable your movement, understand the scientific laws that dictate your momentum and balance. There should be no mystery. But when you dance — when you truly dance — I can’t look away .” “You wanna know the big fucking secret?” She leaned over the table, closing the distance between them. “ It’s called being alive !” His eyebrows angled down, his eyes narrowed, and his jaw bulged, all so slightly that she might have imagined it. But the subtlety of his expression warned her. She backed away from the table . When Ronin moved, it was fast. His chair fell backward as he stood. With an almost casual sweep of his arm, he batted the table away, knocking it onto its side. The gap between them shrank with his relentless advance . Warlord’s scarred face entered her mind. This was close to the way he’d moved, that night . Lara scurried around the counter, desperate for something solid between them. For a terrifying moment, she was convinced he’d smash his way straight through it. She turned away, dropped into a crouch, and raised her arms to shield herself from the coming punishment . “Does a thing have to be flesh and blood to be alive?” he asked, voice low and brimming with anger. “Do humans own that term, that they get to define it ?” She risked a peek at him just as he slammed a fist on the countertop. The crack was loud enough to make her flinch. She stumbled back, a small, frightened sound escaping her throat. Hadn’t she learned anything? Couldn’t she just shut her mouth and dance? It didn’t matter if he enjoyed it; as long as he fed her, why should she care ? “I think, I reason, I react to the world around me. I question what I know and see, and I wonder what the future might bring, though I know it won’t likely be different than the past. I hope !” His voice dwindled, becoming something raw. “I yearn .” Slowly, Lara lowered her arms and looked up at him. His back was to her, hands near his waist with his head angled down toward them. The way he’d sounded just then…it was more human than she’d ever heard him. No matter how many times she tried to convince herself he was just another bot, she knew, at heart, he was different from the gearheads. Different from Warlord . Different from them all . It would have been easy for Ronin to use his fists on her, to batter her flesh and break her bones. Warlord had. But Ronin didn’t so much as touch her. No, it had been her who hurt him . “I’m sorry,” she whispered. Such simple words, with such deep impact. He’d used them when he thought he hurt her. How could she think he was unfeeling ? “I must depart tomorrow.” He turned his head toward her, but didn’t meet her gaze . Her stomach twisted, a
